title: Geissorhiza quadrangula
intro: Geissorhiza quadrangula is a species of flowering plant in the iris family, native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Geissorhiza quadrangula include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Geissorhiza quadrangula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Geissorhiza quadrangula is classified within the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This family consists of a diverse group of flowering plants, many of which are native to southern Africa. Geissorhiza quadrangula is a species of plant within this family. It belongs to the genus Geissorhiza, which comprises approximately 30 species of plants. The classification of Geissorhiza quadrangula is further divided into the subfamily Iridoideae.

It has been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
